title: Comforting, Molten Australian Lamb Pie
description: A hearty, comforting pie filled with slow-cooked lamb, richly spiced with nutmeg and served with a velvety, peppery sauce. This pie is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: In a large pan, heat 1 tablespoon of vegan butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the ground lamb and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the nutmeg, pepper, and all-purpose flour, and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the red wine and beef broth, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the liquid has reduced by half.
Roll out the all-purpose pastry to a thickness of 3mm.
Spoon the lamb mixture into the center of the pastry, leaving a 5cm border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the pastry with a little water, then fold the pastry over the filling to form a pie.
Place the pie in a bain-marie and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
